Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA   95804

RE:  Conflict of Interest --
     Reclaimed Water Plant Expansion

The Clean Water Program (City of San Diego) is proposing to
build a 24 MGD sewer treatment plant at our service and water
district where I am a director. Their proposal stems from a
potential settlement with EPA over inadequate treatment of
sewage outflow.

I have attached a number of articles and agenda items
concerning this item.

Our board will be considering lease terms with "Metro" (City
of San Diego) shortly and I am requesting formal written
ruling on whether I can participate on this item as a
director.

I am also responsible for a 2500 acre development located
immediately adjacent to the proposed plant expansion. A
possible bi-product to the plant expansion is additional
sewer capacity and reclaimed water.

The Panita Ranch development currently has sewer capacity for
it's first phase development which is expected to take a
number of years for completion. However, additional sewer
capacity is needed for the build out of the entire project.

I believe I can participate as a board member because
sufficient existing sewer capacity exists for the next
several years and the primary purpose of the plant expansion
is to satisfy the EPA's demand of "Metro" to upgrade their
sewer treatment to secondary standards.

I understand that the Padre Dam staff intends to ask our
board for conceptual approval of a lease arrangement with
"Metro" at our next regular meeting of March 13. If at all
possible, I'd like to have your guidance by that time. As
indicated, this item will be conceptual only; with the actual
lease coming back at a future date not yet determined.

Thank you.

Douglas S. Wilson
Director

THE CALIFORNIAN
FEBRUARY 4, 1990

Joint forum
will eye
sewer rule

Californian staff report

SANTEE — A joint meeting of
the City Council and the board of
directors for Padre Dam Municipal
Water District will be held Feb. 6,
3:30 p.m. at the district's headquar-
ters, 10887 Woodside Ave.
Among the items scheduled to
be discussed by the city and the
water district is an extension of a
1987 agreement between them that
granted the city control of 415
sewer hook ups.
- The sewer hook up rights must
be bought by developers before
building a project. The city worked
out an agreement with Padre Dam
to control some sewer capacity
when much of Padre Dam's capac-
ity had been sold to developers,
and the city wanted to ensure
enough capacity would remain for
its Town Center redevelopment.
Currently, the city has $28.8 mil-
lion in principal and interest due
on bonds it issued to fund redevel-
opment, which will be paid back
through tax income that is raised
as property values in Town Center
rise. According to a city staff re-
port, property values may not rise
if development in Town Center is
stifled because there is no sewer
capacity left for new development.
- Other items scheduled to be dis-
cussed are water reclamation
projects and programs, the affect
of the Mast Boulevard Bridge ex-
tension project on Santee Lakes,
and water conservation and water
storage and development projects.
- The public is invited to attend
and speak on any item on the
agenda at the meeting. A public
comments portion of the meeting
is scheduled, but the council and
the board are prohibited from act-
ing on any non-agenda item or is-
sue raised during the public com-
ments period.(hbo)

February 1, 1990

TO:     Board of Directors

FROM:   Board Secretary/Executive Assistant

JOINT MEETING WITH SANTEE CITY COUNCIL

     A joint meeting of the Board of Directors and the Santee
City Council is scheduled for Tuesday, February 6, 1990, at
5:30 p.m., to be held in the Board Room of the District Administration Building, 10887 Woodside Avenue, Santee.

     Enclosed for your review prior to the meeting are the
posted agenda for the Board, and related backup material pertaining Item 2 on the agenda.

     Also included are agendas and related backup information for the Santee City Council and Santee Redevelopment
Agency.


                                        Kathryn J. Chichester

KJC

SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY

NOTICE OF AND AGENDA FOR AN ADJOURNED REGULAR MEETING
OF THE SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY
SANTEE, CALIFORNIA
FEBRUARY 6, 1998


THE REGULAR MEETING OF THE SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY CONDUCTED ON JANUARY
24, 1998, WAS ADJOURNED AT ITS CONCLUSION TO TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 6, 5:30 PM, AT
PADRE DAM MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT BOARD ROOM, 12857 WOODSIDE AVENUE, FOR THE
PURPOSE OF A JOINT MEETING WITH THE PADRE DAM MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT BOARD
OF DIRECTORS AND THE SANTEE CITY COUNCIL TO DISCUSS MATTERS OF MUTUAL INTEREST
AS FOLLOWS:

1. RECLAMATION PROJECTS & PROGRAMS.

2. EXTENSION OF THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY/EDWAD SEWER CAPACITY AGREEMENT (See
   staff report Agenda Item 2)

   RECOMMENDATION: Direct staff to prepare documents extending
   the Agreement in Aid of Redevelopment through October, 1993.

3. WATER CONSERVATION.

4. MAST BRIDGE/BLVD. EXTENSION PROJECT.

5. WATER STORAGE TANK & DEVELOPMENT PROJECT.

6. ADJOURNMENT.

ACTION MAY BE TAKEN.

                                    Loretta H. Roper
                                    LORETTA H. ROPER, AGENCY SECRETARY


(SEE SEPARATE AGENDA FOR SANTEE CITY COUNCIL MEETING SAME DATE)

ISS 2/2/90 PM

10765 Woodside Avenue • Santee, California 92071-3198 • (619) 562-6153

City of Santee, California
SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY AGENDA STATEMENT

MEETING DATE February 6, 1990                    AGENDA ITEM NO.    2

ITEM TITLE  EXTENSION OF AGREEMENT IN AID OF REDEVELOPMENT


PREPARED BY Steve Wheeler          DEPARTMENT  City Manager
           Assistant Executive Director
EXPLANATION

     In October of 1987, the Redevelopment Agency Board entered into an
agreement with Padre Dam Municipal Water District giving the Board the authority to
allocate sewer capacity within the Redevelopment Project Area.  The agreement expires
in October, 1990. The Redevelopment Agency's interests would be well served by
extending the agreement for an additional three years, to October, 1993.

     At the present time the Agency has $28,777,000 in principal and interest pay-
ments due on outstanding bonded indebtedness.  Property tax increment is the income
stream that was used to pay the tax allocation bonds that were the source of the
debt. The tax increment will continue to grow only as development takes place in the
Project area. For instance, the Grossmont property contains considerable development
potential - enhanced by the forthcoming Trolley extension.

     A potential constraint on new development in the Project area is the limited
supply of sewer capacity.  We understand that approximately 866 EDUs are available
throughout the district.  Of the original 415 EDUs granted to the agency in 1987, 334
EDUs remain. As we believe this is sufficient to carry the Agency through October 2,
1993, no additional EDU allocation is requested.  Additionally, over the past year we
have taken steps to streamline the application process so there is not an undue wait
for allocation.


Environmental Review

Financial Statement            N/A

                    Continuation of the agreement will help to ensure the Agency
can meet its debt payment obligations.

                                                            Account No.___________

RECOMMENDATION

Direct staff to prepare documents extending the Agreement in Aid of Redevelopment
Through October, 1993.

BOARD/COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION

                                    N/A


ATTACHMENTS (Listed Below)

None

AGREEMENT IN AID OF REDEVELOPMENT BETWEEN
THE SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY AND
PADRE DAM MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT


This agreement is made this 28th day of October 1987 by and
between the SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY, hereinafter referred to
as "Agency", and PADRE DAM MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT, hereinafter
referred to as "District".

RECITALS:

     A.     Agency is a body politic and corporate existing in the
City of Santee ("City") under and by virtue of the California
Redevelopment Law ("Law"); and,

     B.     District exists and operates in San Diego County,
including City's territory under and by virtue of the Municipal
Water District Law of 1911, as amended ("District Law"); and,

     C.     Agency has heretofore adopted the Santee Redevelopment
Plan ("the Plan") for parts of District's territory; and,

     D.     District provides both water and sewer services within
the Plan area; and

     E.     District provides sewer services to the citizens of
Agency and City through a system of limited capacity; and,

     F.     Agency has issued and sold tax increment bonds pursuant
to the Law, to finance redevelopment activities in the redevelop-
ment area; and,

     G.     Repayment of the tax increment bonds is dependent upon
successful redevelopment of Agency's redevelopment areas through
new construction and rehabilitation of existing structures,
thereby increasing real property tax revenues above those from
the base year; and,

     H.     Provision of adequate sanitary sewer service is an
indispensable part of the Plan; and,

     I.     Accomplishment of the Plan is not possible without an
assured ability to provide sewer service and related sewer
capacity through the District's facilities; and,

     J.     District has made a study of its remaining sewer
capacity, both within and without the territory of City and
Agency; and,


                              - 1 -

K.     Analysis of Agency's capacity needs study, and
District's capacity availability study reveal to Agency, City and
District that full implementation of the Plan will exceed
District's remaining, existing capacity; and,

     L.     District and Agency are empowered by law to agree to
act jointly and cooperatively to accomplish the goals of the
Plan; and,

     M.     District desires to act in aid of redevelopment and in
furtherance of the Plan; and,

     N.     Agency desires to join in an agreement of joint action
in aid of redevelopment with District to insure adequate sewer
service and capacity to accomplish the Plan; and,

     O.     District and Agency agree that accomplishment of the
goals of the Plan are of utmost importance to both public
agencies and the citizens thereof; and,

     P.     District and Agency find and determine that this
agreement in aid of redevelopment is a proper exercise of their
respective powers, for a proper public purpose, and an exercise
of their respective governmental powers;

     NOW, THEREFORE, it is hereby agreed as follows:

     Section One.     Findings.     District and Agency agree,
find, determine and declare that the factual statements in the
above recitals are true and correct statements of fact.

     Section Two.     Reservation of Capacity.     District hereby
reserves for use within Agency for redevelopment purposes, as
hereinafter provided, 527 equivalent dwelling units of capacity
in District's existing and future sewer system. If, however,
approval is not given by Agency to the County of San Diego within
30 days of the date of this agreement, pursuant to Section Four
hereof, for the proposed Las Colinas Detention Facility
expansion, the capacity reserved shall be 415 equivalent dwelling
units.

     Section Three.     Allocation of Capacity.     Agency shall
allocate the reserved capacity to accomplish the Plan, as its
sole discretion determines, according to such rules and
regulations as it may, from time to time, deem appropriate, in
order to carry out the redevelopment programs and policies of
Agency.

     Section Four.     Approval by Agency.     District shall not
make any sewer commitment or connect any property to its sewer
system if the property seeking the commitment or sewer service
lies entirely or partly within the territory subject to the Plan

                              - 2 -

(as it now exists, or as it may be lawfully amended) without
first receiving written approval from Agency.

Section Five.     Service in Plan Area.     No sewer
commitment shall be made and no sewer service shall be supplied
by District within any portion of the territory subject to the
Plan unless the person seeking the commitment of service, in
addition to obtaining the written approval of Agency, complies
with all applicable rules and regulations of District, including
the payment of fees and charges and compliance with time
requirements.  Thereafter, District shall provide commitments and
sewer service within the plan area in accordance with its rules
and regulations.

Section Six.     Sewer Capacity Charges.     Agency shall pay
District annually, within 30 days of billing by District, the
amount payable annually by District to the City of San Diego,
pursuant to the Sewage Disposal Agreement of 1974, as amended,
between District and the City of San Diego, which is attributable
to any uncommitted or unused capacity reserved for Agency by
District pursuant to this agreement.

Section Seven.     Hold Harmless.     Agency shall hold and
save harmless District, its directors, officers, employees and
agents, from all costs and losses arising out of or related to
the making, enforcement, and carrying out of this agreement,
including all costs of defense, including reasonable attorneys'
fees, and, if requested by District, shall undertake, at its sole
expense, defense of District, its directors, officers, employees
and agents, in the event of any claim or cause of action is
asserted against District, or any of its directors, officers,
employees, or agents, by virtue of the making, enforcement, or
carrying out this agreement.

Section Eight.     Existing Rights.     Agency and District
agree that this agreement does not affect any property's existing
rights to capacity heretofore committed, and this agreement is
subject to such rights.  No approval of Agency shall be necessary
for the providing of service to any such property.

Section Nine.     Unused Capacity.     Upon termination of
this agreement, any reserved sewer capacity that has not been
committed or utilized shall be subject to distribution by
District without encumbrances or obligations of any kind, and
Agency shall have no right to approve or otherwise affect the
distribution thereof, except as provided by law.

Section Ten.     Termination of Agreement.     This agreement
shall remain in full force and effect for thirty-six (36) months
following its execution by both parties at which time it termi-
nates.  This agreement may be terminated at an earlier date, or
may be extended or modified by the mutual assent of the parties
expressed in writing, duly authorized by the governing bodies of
each party.

- 3 -

Section Eleven.    Operating Memoranda.    The parties may
enter into one or more operating memoranda made by duly author-
ized representatives to implement the terms of this agreement.
Any such operating memoranda shall not effect an amendment to
this agreement.

    Section Twelve.    Representatives.    District shall be
represented in implementing terms of this agreement by its
Manager-Engineer, and Agency shall be represented by its
Executive Director.

    Section Thirteen.    Notices.    Notices sent pursuant to
this agreement shall be addressed as follows:

For the agency:    Ronald L. Ballard
                   Executive Director
                   Santee Redevelopment Agency
                   10765 Woodside Avenue
                   Santee, California 92071-3198

For the District:  E.W. Houser
                   Manager-Engineer
                   Padre Dam Municipal Water District
                   P.O. Box 70
                   Santee, California  92071

    In witness whereof the parties hereto have affixed their
authorized signatures on the dates set forth.


                         SANTEE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY


DATED: ________________    By _____________________________


                         PADRE DAM MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT

DATED: Oct 28 1987        By _____________________________
                             E.W. Houser
                             Manager-Engineer


(DEA2:WPPADR.RA)

Padre Dam Municipal
Water District

10887 Woodside Avenue / P.O. Box 719003
Santee, CA 92072-9003
Telephone: 619-448-3111
FAX Administration: 619-449-9469
FAX Operations: 619-449-9537

Board of Directors:
Douglas S. Wilson
Division 1
Francis G. Daugherty
Division 2
W.E."Baron" Klostermann
Division 3
Wilma R. Strickland
Division 4
G. L. "Jerry" Johnson
Division 5

February 9, 1990

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Request for Advance Ruling--
Conflict of Interest

I am a director on Padre Dam Municipal Water District. I
also am the Vice President in charge of a 2500 acre real
estate master plan development area located within the same
district.

The separately incorporated municipality--City of Santee--
will be asking our Water and Sewer District to extend an
agreement with the City for sewer capacity within their Town
Center area.

Currently, sewer capacity exists to provide immediate demand
for all areas within our sewer district boundaries for the
next several years. However, insufficient capacity exists
on the settle build-out over the future. Prospects are
[illegible] from our district or other from our jurisdiction
of the reclamation plant by our district (an additional 7
million gallons per day) or through construction of a larger
plant (24 million gallons per day) by the City of San Diego's
"Clean Water Program" to comply with EPA's mandate in
settlement of the Federal and State government lawsuit
against the City of San Diego.

The agreement calls for control of 334 EDU's by the City of
Santee for their Town Center development. Our district
currently has 666 EDU's of additional capacity remaining
(beyond that 334 controlled by the city). It has been
predicted that this capacity will more than meet the need of
development for the next few years until additional capacity
becomes available through the programs previously described.

Additionally, the project area I represent has already
purchased 762 EDU's for its first phase development which
won't occur for at least another two years.

I feel that I do not have a conflict given all the above,
but I'm concerned that others may perceive me in competition
with the city for sewer capacity. However, I believe that
sufficient capacity exists to accommodate all until such time
that additional capacity exists.

I am requesting a formal ruling on this issue in advance of
action by the Board. A joint meeting between the Council and
Water Board occurred this week, on Feb. 5, and included
discussion on this item. I did not participate and excused
myself from the room to avoid the potential conflict. This
item will come back and I want to be prepared with a ruling
from you.

Thank you,


Douglas S. Wilson

embedding.qa_text: February 28, 1990 Mr. John Wallace Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 RE: Conflict of Interest -- Reclaimed Water Plant Expansion The Clean Water Program (City of San Diego) is proposing to build a 24 MGD sewer treatment plant at our sewer and water district where I am a director. Their proposal stems from a potential settlement with EPA over inadequate treatment of sewage outflow. I have attached a number of articles and agenda items concerning this item. Our board will be considering lease terms with "Metro" (City of San Diego) shortly and I am requesting formal written ruling on whether I can participate on this item as a director. I am also responsible for a 2500 acre development located immediately adjacent to the proposed plant expansion. A possible bi-product to the plant expansion is additional sewer capacity and reclaimed water. The Fanita Ranch development currently has sewer capacity for it's first phase development which is expected to take a number of years for completion. However, additional sewer capacity is needed for the build out of the entire project. I believe I can participate as a board member because sufficient existing sewer capacity exists for the next several years and the primary purpose of the plant expansion is to satisfy the EPA's demand of "Metro" to upgrade their sewer treatment to secondary standards. I understand that the Padre Dam staff intends to ask our board for conceptual approval of a lease arrangement with "Metro" at our next regular meeting of March 13. If at all possible, I'd like to have your guidance by that time. As indicated, this item will be conceptual only; with the actual lease coming back at a future date not yet determined. Thank you.
